ogTitle: Lennox Head vs Leppington: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lennox Head and Leppington.
heading: Lennox Head vs Leppington — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lennox Head, New South Wales and Leppington, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lennox Head has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Leppington (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lennox Head or Leppington safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lennox Head has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Leppington scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lennox Head scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Leppington scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lennox Head or Leppington?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lennox Head has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
